blocks.content: There was breakfast at the end of the table — the far end. The table is fourteen feet long. I counted once. My parents bought it for a dinner party they've hosted exactly twice.

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: You didn't have to do all this.

blocks.speaker: Larry Jr.
blocks.content: He was up at five. I heard him.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Cole
blocks.content: I know. I heard you in the hall at six.
Mr. Cole hears everything. I stopped being surprised by that sometime around age eleven.

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: He's going to be insufferable when he actually starts school.

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: You're going to tell me something now, aren't you.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Cole
blocks.content: You've had plenty of first days.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Cole
blocks.content: Rooms read you before you open your mouth. They're reading your shoulders, your speed, whether you look at people or through them. You already know how to walk into a room. You've been doing it your whole life.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Cole
blocks.content: I know what it's different from. The principle's the same.
That was it. That was everything he was going to give me. I've learned not to push past it.

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: It means walk like you know where you're going even if you don't.
I almost said something back. Then my phone went off.
